note: Official SNSP data shows lower figures. Noroeste field reporters count 1,972 in first year alone. CNN analysis: homicides rose 400% since El Mayo's capture. Includes April 2026 events: Good Friday (Apr 4) 8 killed, Culiacán rehab center (Apr 1) 8 killed, Escuinapa police ambush (Mar 31) 4 officers killed.
Single worst 30-day death toll in Culiacán's history. Record 30 murders in single day (Sep 17, 2024). City hospitals overwhelmed.

9,000+ families (academic estimate) vs 1,763 families (state official count). At least 50 rural communities abandoned. Improvised camps near Culiacán landfill with shortages of food, water, medical care.

2,133 kidnappings recorded since September 2024. Average 5.3 disappeared per day. Journalists describe disappearances as the 'primary form of lethal violence' in this war. Most bodies never recovered.
Mayor confirmed approximately 100 families (450 people) displaced. Municipality overall population dropped from 37,757 to 26,542 in census data (near 30% reduction including pre-war trends).
261 displaced families documented by mid-2025 — the highest single-municipality count in the conflict. Communities including El Palmito, El Espinal, and others effectively abandoned.
238 displaced families recorded by mid-2025. Multiple factions controlling territory simultaneously makes civilian life untenable in this central Sinaloa municipality.

More than 50 killed in the first seven days following La Mayiza's September 9 offensive. Bodies found with faction symbols (sombreros, pizza slices) as psychological warfare. Schools closed, Independence Day celebrations cancelled.
227% increase over same period in 2024. Tourist hub swept into conflict as factions contest Mazatlán's criminal plaza. Significant impact on hospitality sector occupancy.
Mexican Army kills 19 suspected cartel combatants in the bloodiest single military engagement with narco-traffickers in years. One local commander arrested. Occurred during peak violence period.
Eleven killed in Navy operation targeting Sinaloa Cartel leadership. Plaza boss Omar 'El Patas' Torres captured. High-powered weapons and tactical equipment seized. Torres's daughter released.

At least 3 killed when La Mayiza destroyed a Chapitos armored truck with a .50-caliber rifle in Tierra Blanca, June 15–16, 2025. Occurred just two days after Security Secretary García Harfuch arrived with 1,400 Army troops.
At least 571 civilians killed in Sinaloa in the first eight months of 2025 according to conflict tracking organizations. Includes non-combatants caught in crossfire, extortion victims, and witnesses.

Chapitos gunmen stormed a drug rehabilitation center in Culiacán on April 1, 2026, killing 8 people while searching for a La Mayiza rival believed to be receiving treatment there. Occurred despite active Semana Santa SEDENA/SEMAR security operations. Consistent with a documented pattern of both factions targeting civilian rehabilitation and healthcare facilities to pursue enemies.

A cartel hit squad ambushed municipal police on the Mazatlán–Tepic highway near Tecualilla community in Escuinapa. The operational subdirector of the Escuinapa police command, Esteban Gutiérrez Mazariegos, was among the four killed. One officer critically injured. Occurred despite the active Semana Santa security surge. Escuinapa municipality is a key southern Sinaloa gateway for factions pushing toward Nayarit.

Eight people were murdered in Culiacán on Good Friday, April 4, 2026 — Sinaloa led Mexico with 7 of 64 national intentional homicides that day despite active Semana Santa security operations. Municipal officer Jose Luis, a 4-year veteran, was shot multiple times while on his motorcycle on the Benito Juárez bypass (La Costerita, Punta Azul sector) — the 34th Culiacán officer killed since September 2024. Local journalists attributed killings to ongoing Chapitos–Mayitos faction disputes.
